channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: In Nevada, Trump's policies are making things tough for Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo
item.description: In one of the most competitive races for governor this year, Nevada Democratic Attorney General Aaron Ford will challenge Republican Gov. Joe Lomardo for his office in November.
item.content:encoded:
In one of the most competitive races for governor this year, Nevada Democratic Attorney General Aaron Ford will challenge Republican Gov. Joe Lomardo for his office in November.

(Image credit: Charles Krupa/AP, Ethan Miller/Getty Images

)
item.dc:creator: Paul Boger

item.title: After years of declines, young students show gains in reading and math
item.description: Unscathed by pandemic-era school closures, the nation's 9-year-olds showed progress in math and reading. It's a different story for 13-year-olds, however.
item.content:encoded:
Unscathed by pandemic-era school closures, the nation's 9-year-olds showed progress in math and reading. It's a different story for 13-year-olds, however.

(Image credit: Olivier Touron)
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: Evette, backed by Trump, and Wilson, a Trump supporter, head to S.C. governor runoff
item.description: In South Carolina, both Republican candidates for governor are MAGA devotees, but Trump only endorsed Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette, not Attorney General Alan Wilson. The two are headed to a GOP runoff.
item.content:encoded:
In South Carolina, both Republican candidates for governor are MAGA devotees, but Trump only endorsed Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette, not Attorney General Alan Wilson. The two are headed to a GOP runoff.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Gavin Jackson

item.title: House approves labor-friendly bill with support from 20 Republicans
item.description: The House has approved a bill to slash the time it takes for newly unionized workers to get a first contract. The measure allows for government intervention if a deal is not reached within 90 days.
item.content:encoded:
The House has approved a bill to slash the time it takes for newly unionized workers to get a first contract. The measure allows for government intervention if a deal is not reached within 90 days.

(Image credit: Graeme Sloan)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Three judges, three scandals and new scrutiny of judicial accountability
item.description: Three judges are facing misconduct allegations in three different states, putting pressure on the federal judiciary's system for policing bad behavior in its own ranks.
item.content:encoded:
Three judges are facing misconduct allegations in three different states, putting pressure on the federal judiciary's system for policing bad behavior in its own ranks.

(Image credit: Elijah Nouvelage/Bloomberg via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: A warm World Cup welcome? U.S. immigration policies have chilling effect
item.description: As the World Cup begins later this week, match officials and team members have faced enhanced immigration scrutiny when trying to enter the United States. A FIFA referee from Somalia was turned away.
item.content:encoded:
As the World Cup begins later this week, match officials and team members have faced enhanced immigration scrutiny when trying to enter the United States. A FIFA referee from Somalia was turned away.

(Image credit: Jay Biggerstaff)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: NASA names 4 astronauts on the 'highly complex' Artemis III lunar training mission
item.description: The crew of four — NASA astronaut and commander Randy Bresnik, European Space Agency pilot Luca Parmitano, NASA mission specialists Frank Rubio and Andre Douglas — are scheduled to launch next year.
item.content:encoded:
The crew of four — NASA astronaut and commander Randy Bresnik, European Space Agency pilot Luca Parmitano, NASA mission specialists Frank Rubio and Andre Douglas — are scheduled to launch next year.

(Image credit: Ronaldo Schemidt)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Maine's Senate race and much more. Here are the primary contests to watch today
item.description: Republican incumbents are facing tough challenges in Maine and Nevada. In South Carolina, a crowded field of MAGA-devoted Republicans are facing off to be the next governor.
item.content:encoded:
Republican incumbents are facing tough challenges in Maine and Nevada. In South Carolina, a crowded field of MAGA-devoted Republicans are facing off to be the next governor.

(Image credit: John Locher)
item.dc:creator: The NPR Network

item.title: The Supreme Court is in its final stretch this term. Here are the major cases left
item.description: The Supreme Court is heading into its crunch time, the part of the year when the justices are racing to finish decisions and dissents in the cases that remain undecided. Here's what's left.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court is heading into its crunch time, the part of the year when the justices are racing to finish decisions and dissents in the cases that remain undecided. Here's what's left.

(Image credit: Tyrone Turner)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Staff resignations deepen health care problems at New York jail
item.description: A New York jail is struggling to provide adequate health care and pay medical workers, even after the last health vendor went bankrupt and a new one took over. Now, nurses are resigning.
item.content:encoded: A New York jail is struggling to provide adequate health care and pay medical workers, even after the last health vendor went bankrupt and a new one took over. Now, nurses are resigning.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Izar
